# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2021-01-19
*Regular Meeting — Unofficial Notes*
*Based on auto-generated transcript. Not an official record.*
*Generated 2026-03-28 13:28 UTC*

---

# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees
### Regular Meeting — January 19, 2021

*These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions.*

---

## Opening
- Meeting called to order at **6:00 PM** by President Hubert; quorum confirmed.
- **Invocation and pledges** led by Dale Edmond, who offered a prayer including a blessing for incoming President Biden and Vice President Harris.

---

## Awards & Recognition

### 2A — School Board Recognition Month
- **Dr. Audrey Young**, newly elected State Board of Education member (District 8), addressed the board, thanking trustees for their volunteer service and presenting a certificate of appreciation from the state board.
- **Dr. AJ Livecchi**, Principal of Oak Ridge High School, spoke on behalf of all ~8,500 employees and ~65,000 students, thanking the board for their leadership, particularly during the challenging 2020-2021 school year. Students provided cards, posters, banners, and gifts.
- Multiple board members spoke, expressing humility and gratitude:
  - **President Hubert** emphasized that the educators are "in the trenches" doing the hard work.
  - **Vice President Moore** praised the district, saying "wherever we go in the state... we can hold our heads high."
  - **Mr. Sanders** shared an anecdote about a former trustee suggesting he move to double board member salaries (which are $0), expressing pride in serving.
  - Another board member noted moving to the district 20 years ago specifically for the schools and praised staff investment in students.

### 2B — Grand Oaks High School Improv Team — 2020 State Champions
- **Dr. Chris Povich**, principal of Grand Oaks High School, introduced **Travis Metter**, theater director who founded the program in 2018 as the sole theater teacher.
- **O. Lambert**, team captain, spoke about the team culture that "encourages mistakes."
- Noted as Grand Oaks' **first state championship** — the school will celebrate its first graduating class in 2021.
- Plaque and certificate presented.

### 2C — Robotics and CTE Teachers — COVID-19 Preparedness
- **Chris McCourt**, Executive Director of Operations, recognized teachers and robotics team members who created PPE (masks, face shields, hands-free sanitizer dispensers) during spring/summer 2020.
- Recognized individuals from multiple campuses: Conroe High, Oak Ridge High, Oak Ridge 9th Grade, ACES, College Park, and Texas Torque Robotics.
- **Scott Ripatoe** (retired, Texas Torque coach since 2007) shared that the team distributed approximately **7,500 face shields** across the country, including to Indian reservations. They also designed an intubation chamber and applied for a patent.
- Plaques presented to those in attendance; several recipients were absent (working or retired).

### 2D — Ms. Trudy Skinner — EFTA Dr. Don J. Stockton Sci-Tech Award of Achievement
- **Monica Bomkamp Enia**, President of Education for Tomorrow Alliance (EFTA), presented the 2020 award to **Trudy Skinner**, science teacher at McCullough Junior High.
- Recognized for advisory roles in engineering design competition, Science Bowl, Science Fair, and Sci-Store.
- **Mr. Sanders** emphasized the importance of teachers, students, and partnerships like EFTA in STEM education.

### 2E — Conroe ISD Education Foundation Update
- **Nelda Blair** (Foundation President) and **Maris Blair** (Executive Director) presented the annual report.
- Key highlights despite the pandemic canceling the annual breakfast fundraiser:
  - **$400,000+** in student scholarships since inception
  - **$794,000+** in continuing education scholarships since inception
  - **189 recipients** of continuing education scholarships in 2020 (up from ~130 prior years)
  - **39 paraprofessional scholarships** (record-breaking, up from 6 in the first year)
  - **96 "Welcome Back" gift cards** to CISD graduates returning as teachers ($100 each)
  - **10 "All Means All" freshman scholarships** ($2,000 each, requiring B average and no discipline referrals)
  - **66 campuses and departments** received funds
  - $36,700 raised despite no breakfast event; donors declined refunds
- **2021 Annual Scholarship Breakfast** planned for **Friday, May 7** at Woodforest Stadium

---

## Citizen Participation
- **10 people** signed up to speak.
- **Gwen Allan** spoke regarding a complaint about the Oak Ridge High School cheer program, alleging she was physically threatened by a cheer coach, that her child was being unfairly treated, and that her senior student's schedule was changed without notice in a way that could affect scholarship eligibility. She requested an apology.
  - **Dr. Knoll** directed her to the formal complaint process and introduced **Dr. Winkler** to assist her.

*(Note: Only one citizen speaker's comments were captured in the transcript.)*

---

## Public Hearing — Annual Performance Report
**Dr. Hines** presented highlights of the annual performance report:

- **Rating:** Not rated due to state disaster declaration; no STAAR scores for 2019-2020.
- **Special Education:** Received "Needs Assistance" determination due to missed timelines during school closures.
- **Enrollment:** 64,517 students (end of 2019-2020).
- **Disciplinary Placements:** 10-year trend has leveled off after years of improvement.
- **Demographics:** 80+ home languages; largest non-English is Spanish (~15,000 households).
- **Attendance (2019):** 96.3% (above state average of 95.4%).
- **Dropout Rate (2019):** 0.2% annual rate (grades 9-12); below state average.
- **Graduation Cohort (2019):** 96.4% four-year graduation rate; 97.7% five-year rate.
- **College Readiness:** ~66% scored 3+ on AP exams; 23.3% earned dual credit; 61.3% met SAT/ACT criteria; average SAT score of 1153.
- **Financial:**
  - **Five-Star Smart Schools recipient** for 11 consecutive years
  - Unassigned fund balance: **$153,677,343** (27% of budget)
  - Tax rate: **$1.21** (one of the lowest in the region)
  - Per-student instructional spending: **$8,664** (below most peer districts)
  - Administration costs: **1.78%** of budget (state average: 3.25%)
- **Staff:** 63.6% professional staff; average teacher experience 11 years; turnover rate 13.9%; average salary $59,725.
- **Criminal Incidents:** 37 reportable incidents at 8 campuses (32 were felony controlled substance); all at secondary schools; numbers were down partly due to remote learning.
- **Counselors/Librarians:** 158 counselors, 64 librarians; at least one of each at every campus.
- No public comments were offered on the report.

---

## Consent Agenda (Item 4)
**Vote: Approved unanimously** (no items pulled)

---

## Item 5A — Attendance Zone Boundary Changes for Hope Elementary School
- **Dr. Hines and Chris McCourt** presented Scenario 7.0 for boundary changes associated with opening **Ruben Hope Elementary** (K-4, ~950 capacity, Granger Pines area, opening August 2021).
- Addresses crowding at Austin, Creighton, and anticipated crowding at San Jacinto.
- Key features of Scenario 7.0:
  - San Jacinto opens fall with 400+ students (other scenarios would not achieve this)
  - Maintains bilingual program viability at San Jacinto
  - Hope Elementary opens with 400+ students
  - Economically disadvantaged numbers remain balanced across campuses
  - Sections 26C and 27A move into a clean K-12 Conroe High feeder pattern
  - Recommended by the boundary committee

**Vote: Approved unanimously**

---

## Item 5B — Adoption of 2021-2022 School Calendar
**Dr. Upshaw** presented Calendar C1, recommended by the District Level Planning Committee:
- Meets required 75,600 instructional minutes
- Adds one additional instructional day
- Maintains full week off for Thanksgiving (82% teacher preference)
- Starts mid-week (August 11) — noted this does **not** affect UIL football/band start dates
- Ends before Memorial Day weekend
- First semester ends December 17th (balanced semesters)
- No election day off; that day moved to create a **four-day weekend in April** (82% survey preference)
- Includes makeup weather days and additional staff development days
- Two early release days (December and May)
- Requires renewal of district waiver (to be voted on later in the year)

**Vote: Approved unanimously**

---

## Item 6A — Guaranteed Maximum Price for Safety & Security 2021 Project
**Mr. Foster** presented:
- GMP of **$4,249,687** with Ellisor Constructors (construction manager at risk, selected February 2020)
- Part of 2019 bond district-wide safety and security initiative (annual project)
- Design by PBK architects/engineers

**Vote: Approved unanimously**

---

## Item 6B — Capital Improvement Update (Informational)
**Mr. Foster** provided updates on ongoing construction projects:

| Project | Status | Target Completion |
|---|---|---|
| **Hope Elementary** (Flex School #20) | Exterior masonry nearly complete; interior library taking shape; on schedule | August 2021 |
| **Runyan Elementary** (PE classroom/library addition) | Complete, awaiting fire marshal sign-off; classroom recapture underway | February 2021 |
| **York Junior High** (capacity addition) | ~45% complete; on schedule | August 2021 |
| **College Park High School** (addition to reduce portables) | ~40-45% complete; on schedule | August 2021 |
| **The Woodlands High School** (3-story addition) | ~45% complete; on schedule | August 2021 |
| **Armstrong Elementary** (kitchen/serving expansion + classrooms) | Site development underway; on schedule | August 2021 |
| **Creighton Elementary** (full campus overhaul — mechanical, fire sprinkler, water storage) | Just starting; spans two summers due to water tank lead time | August 2022 |
| **New Junior High** (Candy Creek feeder zone) | 62-acre site cleared; underground utilities/storm drainage underway; includes drainage improvements for broader complex | August 2023 |
| **Conroe High School** (master renovation) | Bus loop/site development and electrical infrastructure underway as headstart; full project GMP expected for board approval next month | 54-month construction schedule |

---

## Item 7A — Award of RFP 20-10-05: School Buses
**Mr. Rice** presented:
- Award to **Thomas Bus Gulf Coast**
- **$2,165,000** estimated expenditure
- Purchasing **15 buses** (77-passenger diesel) and **5 buses** (47-passenger diesel)
- All equipped with **seat belts and AC**
- Average cost: ~$104,000 per bus (77-passenger)
- 10 buses replace vehicles at end of life expectancy; 15 are additions for growth
- Funded from capital projects fund and **HGAC Clean Vehicles Grant Program** (replacing older, less efficient buses)
- Bus life expectancy: typically 15-25 years (some still in service from the 1990s and even 1986)

**Vote: Approved unanimously**

President Hubert expressed appreciation for the transportation department and asked that thanks be conveyed to all staff.

---

## Item 7B — Approval of 2019-2020 Comprehensive Annual Financial Report (CAFR)
**Sarah Roberts** (Weaver and Tidwell, audit partner) presented:
- **Single audit** consisting of financial statement audit and federal awards audit
- **Financial statements:** Unmodified (clean) opinion; no reportable findings
- **Federal awards:** Major programs audited were Title I Part A and ESSER (CARES Act) — combined ~$13.4 million (~1/3 of total federal expenditures); unmodified opinion; no reportable findings
- Previously reviewed by audit committee on January 13, which recommended approval

**Vote: Approved unanimously**

Board members thanked the audit committee and finance staff.

---

## Item 7C — Financial Reports
**Ms. Garza** presented financial statements as of December 31, 2020:
- **General Fund:**
  - Total assets: $434.4 million
  - Cash and investments: $222.2 million (largest portion in state pools at $153.8M; TCG longer-term investments at $52.6M)
  - Total revenue YTD: $207.6 million
  - Total expenditures YTD: $151.9 million
  - Tax collections: 47.08% of levy (on track)
- **2019 Bond:**
  - Encumbered to date: $105.8 million
  - Remaining: $530.6 million
  - Sold to date: $311.5 million of $653.5 million authorized
- **Self-Funded Insurance:**
  - Revenue: $17.8 million; Expenses: $18+ million; Net loss: $284,000
  - Wellness center: 1,396 total visits (349/month average)
- **Investment Portfolio:**
  - Total par value: $606.1 million
  - Pool yield: 0.155%; TCG yield: 1.405% (WAM 472 days)
  - Combined portfolio WAM: 44 days; yield: 2.63%
  - Benchmark (90-day T-bill): 0.075%

---

## Executive Session
- Board entered **closed session at 7:40 PM** under Texas Open Meetings Act sections **551.071** (consultation with attorney) and **551.074** (personnel matters).
- No actions from executive session were recorded in the transcript.

---

## Summary of Votes

| Item | Description | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Item 4 | Consent Agenda | **Approved unanimously** |
| Item 5A | Hope Elementary boundary changes (Scenario 7.0) | **Approved unanimously** |
| Item 5B | 2021-2022 School Calendar (C1) | **Approved unanimously** |
| Item 6A | Safety & Security 2021 GMP ($4,249,687) | **Approved unanimously** |
| Item 7A | School Bus Purchase ($2,165,000) | **Approved unanimously** |
| Item 7B | 2019-2020 CAFR | **Approved unanimously** |

---

*Note: The district calendar waiver renewal was mentioned as a separate future agenda item. The Conroe High School master renovation GMP is expected to come before the board for approval at the next monthly meeting.*
